name: Grid DERMS Attack (Distributed Energy Resources)
description: Attack a Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS) to coordinate manipulation of solar, battery, and EV resources across a utility's distribution grid — causing voltage instability, frequency excursions, or load-generation imbalance.
root_objective: Compromise DERMS to coordinate DER manipulation causing grid instability

nodes.title: Destabilize Distribution Grid via DERMS Compromise
nodes.description: Gain control of the utility's DERMS platform to simultaneously manipulate thousands of distributed energy resources (rooftop solar, battery storage, EV chargers) causing coordinated load swings, voltage violations, or islanding events.

nodes.title: Compromise DERMS Central Platform
nodes.description: Attack the cloud-hosted or on-premise DERMS application (e.g., GE DERMS, Schneider ADMS, Siemens Spectrum Power) to gain fleet-level control of DER assets.

nodes.title: Compromise DER Aggregator APIs
nodes.description: Attack the third-party aggregator APIs (OpenADR, IEEE 2030.5) used to communicate dispatch signals to DER devices.

nodes.title: Exploit DERMS Web Application Vulnerabilities
nodes.description: Find and exploit authentication bypass, SQL injection, or API abuse vulnerabilities in the DERMS web management console to gain admin access.

nodes.title: Compromise Utility User Credentials
nodes.description: Phish or credential-stuff utility engineer accounts that have DERMS dispatch authority to issue fleet-wide control commands.

nodes.title: Issue Coordinated DER Curtailment / Discharge Commands
nodes.description: Use DERMS access to simultaneously curtail all solar generation and discharge all battery storage, creating a massive load-generation imbalance on the distribution feeder.

nodes.title: Intercept and Replay OpenADR Signals
nodes.description: Man-in-the-middle the OpenADR VEN-VTN communication to replay or forge demand-response signals that cause mass DER disconnection.

nodes.title: Spoof IEEE 2030.5 Registration to Inject Rogue DERs
nodes.description: Register rogue virtual DER devices with the aggregator to inject false capacity data, causing DERMS to make incorrect dispatch decisions based on phantom generation.

nodes.title: Manipulate Voltage/Frequency Settings on Inverters
nodes.description: Push modified volt-VAR and frequency-watt response curves to DER inverters that cause them to disconnect simultaneously when grid conditions fluctuate.

nodes.title: DERMS Runs on Standard Web Stack with Known CVEs
nodes.description: The DERMS application runs on Java/Spring or .NET web stacks with dependencies that lag behind security patches due to utility change management processes.

nodes.title: Understanding of Grid Topology and DER Fleet Composition
nodes.description: Attacker must understand which feeders have high DER penetration and what coordinated actions would create the largest load-generation imbalance.

nodes.title: DERMS Connected to Both SCADA/EMS and Cloud APIs
nodes.description: The DERMS platform bridges the utility's internal SCADA/EMS network with public-facing DER aggregation APIs, providing a pivot path from internet to grid operations.
